content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS of length nnn and an integer KKK.

We will define F(L)F(L)F(L) here. Let's choose KKK random sub-strings of length LLL from SSS.

Then F(L)F(L)F(L) equals the probability such that all of them are pairwise identical.

See the **Explanation** section for more clarity.

You have to find F(L)F(L)F(L) for each 1в‰¤Lв‰¤n1 \leq L \leq n1в‰¤Lв‰¤n.

It can be shown that any F(L)F(L)F(L) can be expressed as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QPвЂ‹, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers, Pв‰Ґ0P \geq 0Pв‰Ґ0, Q>0Q > 0Q>0 and QQQ is coprime with 998244353998244353998244353. You should compute PГ—Qв€’1modвЂ‰вЂ‰998244353P \times Q^{-1} \mod 998244353PГ—Qв€’1mod998244353, where Qв€’1Q^{в€’1}Qв€’1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

First line will contain two integers, nnn (the length of string SSS) and KKK.
Next line will contain the string SSS.


Output Format
Print nnn space separated integers, F(L)F(L)F(L), for each 1в‰¤Lв‰¤n1 \leq L \leq n1в‰¤Lв‰¤n, in a single line.
constraints: ItвЂ™s guaranteed that SSS contains only lower-case Latin letters.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In our sample, for L=3L= 3L=3, we have 3 sub-strings:

abaabaaba (Starting from 1st position)
babbabbab (Starting from 2nd position)
abaabaaba (Starting from 3rd position)

Among them we can choose in the following ways, to get all 3 of them pairwise identical:

(1,1,1):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(1, 1, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,1,1):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(2,2,2):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(2, 2, 2) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(2,2,2):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(3,3,3):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(3, 3, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,3,3):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(1,1,3):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(1, 1, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,1,3):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(1,3,1):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(1, 3, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,3,1):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(3,1,1):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(3, 1, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,1,1):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(3,3,1):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(3, 3, 1) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,3,1):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(3,1,3):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(3, 1, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(3,1,3):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹
(1,3,3):ProbabilityВ =В 13Г—13Г—13=127(1, 3, 3) : Probability\ =\ \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{27}(1,3,3):ProbabilityВ =В 31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹Г—31вЂ‹=271вЂ‹

(1,3,3)(1, 3, 3)(1,3,3) means that the three random substrings chosen are the substring starting at index 1, at index 3 and at index 3.
So F(3)=9Г—127=13F(3) = 9 \times \frac{1}{27} = \frac{1}{3}F(3)=9Г—271вЂ‹=31вЂ‹
